description_long: This object consists of two pears, each with a distinct elongated shape and a slightly curved stem. The pears display a mottled skin with a mix of yellow-green and brown hues, indicating ripeness and some natural blemishes. The surface texture appears matte with subtle roughness, and the skin shows areas of russeting and minor bruising. The stems are slender and dark, contrasting with the lighter body of the fruit. The pears are positioned close together, with one standing more upright and the other resting on its side. The overall appearance suggests they are mature, edible, and have been handled or stored for a short period.
description: Two ripe pears with mottled yellow-green and brown skin, one upright and one lying on its side, showing natural blemishes and a matte, slightly rough surface.
description_view_0: View 0 shows both pears, with the upright pear in front and the other pear partially behind, highlighting the elongated shape and mottled brown and green skin.
description_view_1: View 1 presents the upright pear facing forward, with the second pear mostly obscured behind, emphasizing the smooth curvature and color gradient.
description_view_2: View 2 displays the side of the upright pear and the rounded base of the second pear, revealing more of the brownish area and the stem of the lying pear.
description_view_3: View 3 focuses on the pear lying on its side, with the upright pear in the background, showing the full length and surface blemishes of the horizontal pear.
attribute_contextual_placement: resting on its side or upright in a fruit display or bowl
attribute_condition: ripe with some blemishes and minor bruising
attribute_orientation: one pear upright with stem upward, the other lying on its side
